relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The service for the holy ones is already known.
The siblings go ahead so the readiness is real.
Prepared generosity protects everyone from shame.
The gift should be blessing, not pressured greed.
Sowing sparingly and sowing blessing shape different harvests.
The gift is chosen in the heart, not dragged out by grief or necessity.
The Divine can make grace abound into all sufficiency for every good work.
Scattered generosity to the poor remains as righteousness/justice.
The seed-giving Divine multiplies the fruits of justice.
Enrichment becomes generosity, and generosity becomes thanksgiving.
The service fills lack and overflows into thanks.
Generosity proves the good news confession.
Prayer and longing arise from the grace seen upon them.
Thanks to the Divine for the indescribable gift.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 9 completes the giving theology: no compulsion, no scarcity spell, but cheerful grace that becomes sufficiency, justice, and thanksgiving.
